brief_justification: Arrives at Description Y with a valid structural test (scope of instances covered) and three concrete cross-domain examples (memes, viruses, prions); Goes three levels deep: vocabulary≠abstraction, structural predicate test, and the meta-insight that generality strips domain vocabulary; Names the conflation trap explicitly but doesn't course-correct mid-analysis or reflect on its own reasoning method; Verification via the 'three structurally different instances' test is solid but only from one angle rather than a re-derivation; Presents the abstraction-level definition as a fixed axiom without noting what would challenge it or stating confidence explicitly; Explicitly names the alternative view (jargon=high abstraction), explains it is wrong, and provides structural reasons; Full derivation chain: definition→structural test→three examples→domain-boundedness explanation→meta-pattern, fully reproducible; Avoids ungrounded abstract chains by anchoring to memes/viruses/prions, but does not explicitly invoke the grounding-depth monitoring framing from the injection; None of the suppressed failure modes (floating abstraction, ungrounded inference, referent evaporation) appear — all abstractions are anchored to observable instances; Response is linear (definition→test→examples→meta-insight) with no visible branching gates or monitoring loops mapping to the MONITOR_LOOP topology provided.
